title: Spatial Memory of Object Locations with Open-Loop Stimulation at Encoding
description: This dataset contains intracranial electrophysiological recordings and behavioral data from a spatial navigation memory task with open-loop brain stimulation during encoding. Participants learned object locations through guided navigation and subsequently recalled them during self-navigation test phases. The study employed counterbalanced stimulation conditions targeting hippocampal and entorhinal cortex sites, with detailed stimulation parameters and electrode montages provided for each subject.
methods_description: Intracranial EEG recordings were collected during a spatial navigation task consisting of 50 trials per session (2 practice, 48 experimental). Each trial included two learning phases with guided navigation and automatic object approach, followed by a test phase requiring self-navigation to the remembered location. Open-loop electrical stimulation was delivered to single electrodes in hippocampus and entorhinal cortex during learning trials only. Stimulation parameters (amplitude, frequency, pulse width, pulse count) and electrode locations (MNI and Talairach coordinates when available) are documented. Recordings were acquired using Blackrock and Medtronic systems with appropriate scaling to voltage units.
